articleRattanThickness.meta_description: How to pick PE rattan thickness and profile for furniture: load zones, cross-section shape, colour batching, and yield.
articleRattanThickness.title: Choosing rattan thickness for furniture
articleRattanThickness.lead: A practical take: profile shape, structural load, and keeping colour consistent across coils.
articleRattanThickness.intro: Synthetic (PE) rattan is sold by the coil and priced by weight: thicker profiles are usually stiffer and less bendy. For furniture, the millimetres on the spec sheet matter together with the cross-section — half-round, flat, round, oval — and whether the weave carries body load (seat, arm) or is decorative trim.
articleRattanThickness.sec1_a: Areas that bear body weight — seats, loaded arms — usually need a thicker or stiffer profile with even geometry. Decorative rings, overlays, and fine mesh can use thinner material where neat weaving matters more than bending resistance on a long span.
articleRattanThickness.sec1_b: If you hesitate between two neighbouring thicknesses for a large surface, test sag on a mock-up with the same geometry. On a long span a couple of millimetres is more noticeable than on a short run.
articleRattanThickness.sec2_a: Half-round is common for classic shapes and radii; flat suits flat planes and ribbon-style weaving; round works for fine repeating coils. Oval changes pitch and pattern — read shape and thickness together on the product card.
articleRattanThickness.sec2_b: Outdoors, confirm UV stabilisation and allowed conditions for the SKU; indoors, colour consistency across coils on repeat orders is usually the bigger risk.
articleRattanThickness.sec3_a: One batch number for the whole job reduces two-shade mismatches in one piece. Estimate yield by coil length and overlap; a short trial piece on your actual weaving setup validates both flexibility and real consumption.
articleRattanThickness.sec3_b: Start with a small trial coil when you change series or supplier — you check bend feel and whether the gauge matches your fixtures.
articleRattanThickness.outro: In the Bententrade catalogue each item lists thickness, profile shape, colour family, and stock. Need a matched set of profiles for a chair or table? Send span, load, and a sketch via contacts — we’ll advise from workshop experience.

home.strip_title: Why PE rattan works indoors and out
home.strip_sub: Same polymer family as outdoor wicker: flexible, handles moisture, straightforward care.
home.strip_pe: PE stays flexible and strong — suited to furniture and decor that bends under load.
home.strip_uv: UV stabilization helps color last longer in sun under normal use.
home.strip_outdoor: Patios, winter gardens, humid rooms — the polymer doesn’t swell like cellulose-based materials.
home.strip_care: Dust and marks — soft damp cloth; skip acetone and harsh solvents.

product.material_intro: Synthetic rattan based on polyethylene (PE) — the usual choice for outdoor furniture and decor.
product.material_b1: No painting needed; long UV exposure may create a natural patina, like most PE goods.
product.material_b2: Does not absorb water; wipe dry after rain if you like.
product.material_b3: Keep away from open flame; avoid acetone/solvents — they can damage the polymer.

wholesale.title: Wholesale
wholesale.lead: Stable color batches, clear paperwork, and a separate flow from retail. PDF quote after volume and profile are confirmed.
wholesale.benefit_1_body: We align colour and series so the whole project batch looks consistent — fewer rejects and rework on the floor.
wholesale.benefit_2_body: Invoice, specification, and supporting docs in the standard flow; extra formats available under contract.
wholesale.benefit_3_body: Volumes and timelines are handled outside the retail queue — faster alignment and predictable dispatch stages.
wholesale.step1: Short request: volume, profile, target colors, and on-site deadline.
wholesale.step2: We align batch and samples; one batch number for the whole order when volume allows.
wholesale.step3: Invoice, payment as agreed, shipment with supporting documents.
wholesale.form_note: Demo form on the site — wire it to email, Telegram, or your CRM in production.
wholesale.cases: HoReCa and large retail references — ask your manager.

export_page.title: Export B2B
export_page.lead: USD/EUR invoices, logistics, and duty ballparks — calculated for destination country and Incoterms.
export_page.checklist_title: What we align before the invoice
export_page.checklist_1: Incoterms and handover point — they drive insurance and liability on the route.
export_page.checklist_2: Line items and gross weight — for customs description and container or truck choice.
export_page.checklist_3: Currency and payment form — prepayment or documents against payment as agreed.
export_page.checklist_4: Production lead time and colour batch hold — fixed before invoicing.
export_page.quote_checkout: Quote and calculation first — payment after terms are approved.
export_page.form_note: Fields for orientation; final quote and invoice follow from the manager.
